paragraph_outline.topic: Model collapse theory and empirical confirmation
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: The phenomenon of model collapse—where generative AI systems progressively degrade when trained on their own outputs—has emerged as a fundamental threat to the sustainability of AI development.
While researchers have proposed various strategies to mitigate model collapse, these approaches reveal fundamental limitations and new vulnerabilities.
Compounding the model collapse crisis, current approaches to detect AI-generated content systematically fail, creating an untrackable pollution problem.

writing_notes: Key tension to address: The inevitability of collapse (theoretical proofs) versus the optimism of proposed solutions (which all have fatal flaws). Emphasize how detection failure makes the problem unmanageable—we can't even track the pollution. The narrative arc moves from establishing the core problem, through failed solutions, to the compounding crisis of undetectability.

paragraph_outline.topic: Temporal analysis methodologies reveal structural degradation
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Recent empirical work has established methodologies for detecting and quantifying degradation patterns in text corpora over time.

paragraph_outline.topic: Domain-specific evidence of persistent quality decline
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: These measurement frameworks reveal consistent degradation patterns across diverse textual domains spanning centuries to decades.

paragraph_outline.topic: Computational analysis confirms semantic and performance degradation
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Computational approaches further confirm these degradation patterns through declining performance metrics and semantic coherence measures.

writing_notes: Key tension to address: Balance between showing historical precedent for degradation (astronomy texts) and contemporary urgency (digital media, sentiment analysis). The narrative arc moves from methodology establishment to historical evidence to modern computational confirmation.

spine.transition_to_next: This entropy-diversity balance becomes crucial when analyzing semantic coherence...

spine.role_in_narrative: Shows how different computational models reveal distinct semantic patterns
spine.transition_to_next: These semantic loosening patterns can be combined with information measures for comprehensive authenticity assessment...

Shows practical implementation of multi-modal signal integration

paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Recent theoretical advances have established rigorous connections between information measures and linguistic structure.
Building on these information-theoretic foundations, researchers have explored how entropy and diversity measures interact in computational text analysis.
These theoretical insights converge toward a unified framework for authenticity detection that combines multiple complementary signals.

writing_notes: Key tension to address: balancing mathematical rigor with practical applicability. The narrative must show how abstract information theory concepts (entropy, mutual information) connect to concrete text quality measures (TTR, semantic coherence) and ultimately support the L_auth formula.

anchor_paper.why: Establishes the core validation premise that current LLMs trained on web data lack genuine social intelligence, providing the baseline against which human-verified social data can be tested

spine.role_in_narrative: Establishes baseline: GPT-3 lacks Theory of Mind despite massive web training
spine.transition_to_next: This fundamental limitation in web-trained models prompted deeper investigation into the relationship between data quality and social reasoning capabilities...

spine.role_in_narrative: Quantifies misalignment between LLM outputs and human demographic groups
spine.transition_to_next: Given this systematic misalignment, researchers began exploring whether the problem stemmed from training data quality rather than model architecture...

spine.role_in_narrative: Proves data curation matters more than architecture for model success
spine.transition_to_next: This insight catalyzed a shift toward human-in-the-loop approaches for data curation and model alignment...

spine.role_in_narrative: DPO shows direct preference optimization without separate reward models
spine.transition_to_next: Building on DPO's success, subsequent work explored whether human feedback could be systematically integrated into the training pipeline...

spine.role_in_narrative: AlpacaFarm demonstrates cost-effective human feedback simulation
spine.transition_to_next: These advances in feedback mechanisms enabled researchers to test whether verified social data could overcome the limitations identified in web-trained models...

spine.role_in_narrative: Reveals Clever Hans effects in LLM social reasoning tasks
spine.transition_to_next: This critical finding underscores the importance of carefully curated, human-verified data for genuine social intelligence rather than surface-level pattern matching.

supporting.role: Reinforces Theory of Mind limitations through sycophancy analysis
Shows dissociation between self-reported and actual behavior
Exemplifies successful data curation strategy at scale
Yi models confirm data quality as primary performance driver
RLAIF shows AI feedback can match human feedback quality
Zephyr demonstrates DPO effectiveness at 7B scale
Process supervision outperforms outcome supervision for reasoning
MoRAgent shows role decomposition improves agent capabilities

paragraph_outline.topic: Web-trained models fail at genuine social reasoning
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Despite massive scale, language models trained on web-scraped data consistently fail to demonstrate genuine social intelligence and Theory of Mind capabilities.

paragraph_outline.topic: Data curation quality determines model capabilities
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Recent empirical evidence demonstrates that data curation strategies, rather than model architectures, primarily determine downstream performance on complex reasoning tasks.

paragraph_outline.topic: Human feedback enables verified social reasoning
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Direct preference optimization and human-in-the-loop approaches have emerged as effective methods for aligning models with genuine human social reasoning patterns.

writing_notes: Key tension to address: The validation experiments must clearly distinguish between surface-level pattern matching (Clever Hans effects) and genuine social reasoning capabilities. The narrative should build from identifying the problem (models lack ToM) through understanding the root cause (data quality) to demonstrating the solution (human-verified data with preference optimization).

paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: The model collapse phenomenon has moved from theoretical concern to empirical reality, creating an urgent need for platforms that can authenticate and curate training data.

paragraph_outline.topic: Self-correction and adversarial risks in curation
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: While self-correction mechanisms and human curation offer pathways to stability, they also introduce new vulnerabilities that require authentication infrastructure.

paragraph_outline.topic: Proven platform architectures for authenticated data
paragraph_outline.opening_sentence: Existing computational science platforms demonstrate the feasibility of combining provenance tracking, trust mechanisms, and metadata packaging at scale—capabilities that directly address the authentication challenges identified in the collapse literature.

writing_notes: Key tension to address: Bridge the gap between theoretical collapse prevention (category A papers) and concrete platform implementations (category G papers). Show how CampusGo synthesizes insights from both domains into a unified authentication framework. Emphasize that while collapse papers identify the problem and curation as a solution, they lack the platform infrastructure that computational science has already developed.
